title: Polalrized reservoirs in dynamics of polariton condensation
authors: I. A. Shelykh, A. V. Yulin
abstract: We review the problem of description of the dynamics of driven-disspipative spinor polariton condensates, focusing on the terms corresponding to the coupling between a macroscopic wavefunction of the condensdate and incoherent excitonic reservoir created by a non-resonant pump. We demonstrate that the existing version of the theory breaks down in case, when reservoir has non-zero components of the Stokes vector corresponding to in-plane linear polarization. The polarization invariant theory of reservoir to condensate coupling is formulated with use of the spin density matrix formalism.
summary: This paper develops a rigorous theoretical framework using the spin density matrix formalism to describe the dynamics of spinor polariton condensates coupled to an excitonic reservoir. It corrects flaws in existing theories concerning polarization invariance and analyzes how the condensate's polarization state evolves under external pumping, showing the critical role of reservoir relaxation times.
main_problem: The paper addresses the theoretical description of dynamics in driven-dissipative spinor polariton condensates, specifically focusing on the coupling between the condensate and an incoherent excitonic reservoir.
main_result: The standard theory fails when the reservoir has non-zero in-plane linear polarization; the correct description requires using the spin density matrix formalism to maintain polarization invariance.
method: The authors derive and analyze coupled equations for the condensate and reservoir density matrices using the spin density matrix formalism, transforming between circular and linear polarization bases.
model_or_system: The system involves cavity polaritons, which are modeled as spinor condensates coupled to an incoherent excitonic reservoir. The dynamics are governed by coupled equations derived from a mean-field approach.
key_observables: Condensate wavefunction ($\Psi$), condensate density matrix ($\hat{ho}$), reservoir density matrix ($\hat{ho}_R$), and the Stokes parameters ($S_x, S_y, S_z$) characterizing the condensate polarization.
important_parameters: Relaxation time $	au_{\parallel}$, pump polarization components ($S_x, S_y, S_z$), and coupling constants ($g, 	ilde{g}, \gamma, \Gamma$).
main_assumptions:
The analysis often neglects spatial effects ($
abla^2\phi$ term) and assumes the coupling is limited to polaritons and incoherent excitons.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the stationary state polarization dependence on pump ellipticity ($S_P z$), showing polarization plane rotation above a threshold, and the time evolution of Stokes parameters, which are non-sinusoidal near threshold but approach sinusoidal behavior for large $S_P z$.
paper_structure: The paper first establishes the theoretical necessity of the spin density matrix formalism due to polarization invariance issues. It then applies this formalism to analyze the polarization dynamics of the condensate under varying pump ellipticity and reservoir relaxation times.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant to AMO physics and quantum optics as it tackles the complex, non-equilibrium dynamics of interacting quantum matter waves (polaritons) in the presence of dissipation and polarization effects.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
